title: Hyperactivity and Inattention - ADHD
chief_complaint: 8-year-old boy with difficulty paying attention in school, easily distracted, fidgety, cannot sit still, interrupts others, and forgets instructions. Symptoms present for 2 years, affecting academic performance and social interactions.

physical_exam.neuro: Normal neurological examination, no tics, no motor abnormalities

hidden_data.vanderbilt: Vanderbilt Assessment: Positive - 7/9 inattention symptoms, 8/9 hyperactivity symptoms in multiple settings (home and school)

correct_diagnosis: Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD), Combined Type
key_learning_points: DSM-5: symptoms in 2+ settings for >6 months
Vanderbilt scales from parents and teachers

patient_responses.school: Teacher says he can't stay in his seat, blurts out answers
patient_responses.home: He can't follow multi-step instructions, loses things
patient_responses.social: Other kids don't want to play because he's too rough

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! ADHD combined type with classic symptoms.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is ADHD. The Vanderbilt scales confirm symptoms in multiple settings.
feedback.attending_note: ADHD management: Behavioral parent training + classroom accommodations. Methylphenidate (Ritalin) 5mg BID starting dose, titrate up. Monitor growth (height, weight), appetite, sleep. Regular Vanderbilt follow-ups. Combined treatment (behavioral + medication) most effective.
